metadata.title: Compute Constraint (B1)
metadata.description: Pushing capability raises compute demand, which raises scarcity and cost, which throttles capability growth — for everyone, including the leader.
metadata.author: Tracking AI Course

nodes.label: Model Capability
nodes.description: Aggregate quality of the frontier model.

nodes.label: Compute Demand
nodes.description: Compute needed to train and serve increasingly large models.

nodes.label: Compute Scarcity / Cost
nodes.description: Market price and queue depth for advanced GPUs and power capacity.

nodes.label: Training Throughput
nodes.description: Effective training compute available to the lab after costs and queues.

edges.description: Pushing capability requires more compute per training run.
Higher demand raises the price and queue depth for compute.
Scarce, expensive compute reduces effective training throughput.
Lower training throughput slows capability gains.

loops.label: B1: Compute Constraint
loops.description: The hardest physical limit on AI scaling. Slows everyone, but slows capital-poor labs more.
loops.behavior_pattern: Goal-seeking — the system finds an equilibrium between demand and supply of compute.
